Title: A Sensible Person's Guide to Running Nowhere

Thousands of our neighbors rose before dawn, pinned numbers to their chests, and ran twenty-six
miles through the summer heat only to end up more or less where they began. They call it an
achievement; I call it a very elaborate way to arrive back at breakfast. Still, one admires the
commitment, if only from the shaded comfort of a folding chair with a cold drink in hand.

Subject context:
ARTICLE 1: Thousands of runners finished the city's annual marathon in record heat, cheered on
by crowds lining the downtown route.
